title: @container
description: Style elements based on their parent container's size — not the viewport. The same component can adapt its layout wherever it's placed, regardless of screen size.
html:
220px container

Stacked

Container too narrow for row layout

380px container

Row layout

Container has enough space
challenge: The `@container` rule is already written — but it won't fire until `.wrapper` declares itself as a container. Add `container-type: inline-size` to `.wrapper` and watch the wider card switch to a row layout.

alternatives.name: container-type: size
alternatives.description: Use size when a component also needs to respond to its container's height. inline-size is the right default — size is for components placed inside fixed-height containers where vertical space also constrains the layout.

realWorldUsage: The real payoff is truly reusable components. A card placed in a narrow sidebar looks different from the same card in a wide main content area — one component, no JavaScript, no separate variants for different contexts. Design systems are increasingly shipping components with built-in container queries so they adapt to wherever they're placed without any external configuration.
